target.name: Q-Factor

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Q-Factor is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised $24M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 total comparable companies—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method here. Using Q-Factor's $24M total raised as a proxy for seed-stage valuation and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to project next-stage value yields $60M. This represents the most direct triangulation available given the company's stage and the narrow peer set constraints.

Peer-median comparison cannot be reliably applied: while five quantum computing peers exist in the database, none meet the minimum threshold of three comparables when filtered by same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise given the wide dispersion in quantum computing company maturity and funding profiles.

The triangulated valuation range is $51M (low) to $69M (high), with a median of $60M. This single-method estimate carries material uncertainty around quantum computing-specific comparable multiples at seed stage and execution risk inherent to the sector. Validation through follow-on financing activity or narrower peer clustering at later stages would strengthen conviction.
